IfcWall.name: Wall
IfcWall.definition: The wall represents a vertical construction that may bound or subdivide spaces. Wall are usually vertical, or nearly vertical, planar elements, often designed to bear structural loads. A wall is however not required to be load bearing.

properties.name: Acoustic Rating
properties.definition: Technical note: in IFC this property takes 'IfcLabel' as value. Such objects are not included in bSDD for simplicity reason. IFC also doesn't enforce particular units, but recommends using metric SI units (metre, kilogram, etc.). Read the IFC documentation for more information.

properties.name: Status
properties.definition: [[Status]] of the element, predominately used in renovation or retrofitting projects. The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition, 'Existing' - element exists and remains, 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished, 'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure).

allowedValues.description: 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished
The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition
'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure)

properties.name: Gross Foot Print Area
properties.definition: [[Area]] of the wall as viewed by a ground floor view, not taking any wall modifications (like recesses) into account. It is also referred to as the foot print of the wall.

properties.name: Gross Side Area
properties.definition: [[Area]] of the wall as viewed by an elevation view of the middle plane of the wall. It does not take into account any wall modifications (such as openings).

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Gross Weight
properties.definition: Total Gross [[Weight]] of the object without any add-on parts and not taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Height
properties.definition: Characteristic height

properties.name: Length
properties.definition: The length of the object.

properties.name: Net Foot Print Area
properties.definition: [[Area]] of the wall as viewed by a ground floor view, taking all wall modifications (like recesses) into account. It is also referred to as the foot print of the wall.

properties.name: Net Side Area
properties.definition: [[Area]] of the object as viewed by an elevation view of the middle plane of the object. It does take into account all object modifications (such as openings).

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Weight
properties.definition: Total net weight of the object without add-on parts, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Width
properties.definition: The width of the object. Only given, if the object has constant thickness (prismatic).

properties.name: Assembly Place
properties.definition: Enumeration defining where the assembly is intended to take place, either in a factory, other offsite location or on the building site.

allowedValues.description: other offsite location or on the building site
other offsite location or on the building site
other offsite location or on the building site

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Object Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Global ID
properties.definition: Assignment of a globally unique identifier within the entire software world.

properties.name: Name
properties.definition: Optional name for use by the participating software systems or users. For some subtypes of [[IfcRoot]] the insertion of the [[Name]] attribute may be required. This would be enforced by a where rule.

IfcDoor.name: Door
IfcDoor.definition: The door is a built element that is predominately used to provide controlled access for people, goods, animals and vehicles. It includes constructions with hinged, pivoted, sliding, and additionally revolving and folding operations.

properties.name: Status
properties.definition: [[Status]] of the element, predominately used in renovation or retrofitting projects. The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition, 'Existing' - element exists and remains, 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished, 'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure).

properties.name: Height
properties.definition: Characteristic height

properties.name: Peri Meter
properties.definition: [[Perimeter]] of the object.

properties.name: Width
properties.definition: The width of the object. Only given, if the object has constant thickness (prismatic).

properties.name: Operation Type
properties.definition: [[Type]] of operations. Also used to assign standard symbolic presentations according to national building standards.

properties.name: Overall Height
properties.definition: Overall measure of the height, it reflects the Z Dimension of a bounding box, enclosing the body of the door opening. If omitted, the [[OverallHeight]] should be taken from the geometric representation of the [[IfcOpeningElement]] in which the door is inserted.

properties.name: Overall Width
properties.definition: Overall measure of the width, it reflects the X Dimension of a bounding box, enclosing the body of the door opening. If omitted, the [[OverallWidth]] should be taken from the geometric representation of the [[IfcOpeningElement]] in which the door is inserted.

properties.name: User Defined Operation Type
properties.definition: Designator for the user defined operation type, shall only be provided, if the value of [[OperationType]] is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Para Meter Takes Precedence
properties.definition: The Boolean value reflects, whether the parameter given in the attached lining and panel properties exactly define the geometry (TRUE), or whether the attached style shape take precedence (FALSE). In the last case the parameter have only informative value. If not provided, no such information can be inferred.

properties.name: Tag
properties.definition: The tag (or label) identifier at the particular instance of a product, e.g. the serial number, or the position number. It is the identifier at the occurrence level.

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Object Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Description
properties.definition: Optional description, provided for exchanging informative comments.

properties.name: Global ID
properties.definition: Assignment of a globally unique identifier within the entire software world.

properties.name: Name
properties.definition: Optional name for use by the participating software systems or users. For some subtypes of [[IfcRoot]] the insertion of the [[Name]] attribute may be required. This would be enforced by a where rule.

IfcWindow.name: Window
IfcWindow.definition: The window is a building element that is predominately used to provide natural light and fresh air. It includes vertical opening but also horizontal opening such as skylights or light domes. It includes constructions with swinging, pivoting, sliding, or revolving panels and fixed panels. A window consists of a lining and one or several panels.

The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition, 'Existing' - element exists and remains, 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished, 'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure).

allowedValues.description: 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished
The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition
'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure)

properties.name: Overall Height
properties.definition: Overall measure of the height, it reflects the Z Dimension of a bounding box, enclosing the body of the door opening. If omitted, the [[OverallHeight]] should be taken from the geometric representation of the [[IfcOpeningElement]] in which the door is inserted.

properties.name: Overall Width
properties.definition: Overall measure of the width, it reflects the X Dimension of a bounding box, enclosing the body of the door opening. If omitted, the [[OverallWidth]] should be taken from the geometric representation of the [[IfcOpeningElement]] in which the door is inserted.

properties.name: User Defined Partitioning Type
properties.definition: Designator for the user defined partitioning type, shall only be provided, if the value of [[PartitioningType]] is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Para Meter Takes Precedence
properties.definition: The Boolean value reflects, whether the parameter given in the attached lining and panel properties exactly define the geometry (TRUE), or whether the attached style shape take precedence (FALSE). In the last case the parameter have only informative value. If not provided, no such information can be inferred.

properties.name: Tag
properties.definition: The tag (or label) identifier at the particular instance of a product, e.g. the serial number, or the position number. It is the identifier at the occurrence level.

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Object Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Description

properties.name: Global ID
properties.definition: Assignment of a globally unique identifier within the entire software world.

properties.name: Name

IfcSlab.name: Slab
IfcSlab.definition: A slab is a component of the construction that may enclose a space vertically. The slab may provide the lower support (floor) or upper construction (roof slab) in any space in a building.

properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Gross Weight
properties.definition: Total Gross [[Weight]] of the object without any add-on parts and not taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Length
properties.definition: The length of the object.

properties.name: Net Area
properties.definition: Total net area of the object. Openings, recesses and cut-outs are taken into account by subtraction, projections by addition.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Weight

properties.name: Peri Meter

properties.name: Width

properties.name: Assembly Place

properties.name: Tag
properties.definition: The tag (or label) identifier at the particular instance of a product, e.g. the serial number, or the position number. It is the identifier at the occurrence level.

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Object Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Description
properties.definition: Optional description, provided for exchanging informative comments.

properties.name: Global ID
properties.definition: Assignment of a globally unique identifier within the entire software world.

properties.name: Name
properties.definition: Optional name for use by the participating software systems or users. For some subtypes of [[IfcRoot]] the insertion of the [[Name]] attribute may be required. This would be enforced by a where rule.

IfcBeam.name: Beam
IfcBeam.definition: An [[IfcBeam]] is typically a horizontal, or nearly horizontal, structural member that is capable of withstanding load primarily by resisting bending. It may also represent such a member from an architectural point of view. It is not required to be load bearing.

properties.name: Status
properties.definition: [[Status]] of the element, predominately used in renovation or retrofitting projects. The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition, 'Existing' - element exists and remains, 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished, 'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure).

allowedValues.description: 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished
The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition
'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure)

properties.name: Thermal Transmittance
properties.definition: Technical note: in IFC this property takes 'IfcThermalTransmittanceMeasure' as value. Such objects are not included in bSDD for simplicity reason. IFC also doesn't enforce particular units, but recommends using metric SI units (metre, kilogram, etc.). Read the IFC documentation for more information.

properties.name: Cross Section Area
properties.definition: Total area of the cross section (or profile) of the object.

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Gross Weight
properties.definition: Total Gross [[Weight]] of the object without any add-on parts and not taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Length
properties.definition: The length of the object.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Weight
properties.definition: Total net weight of the object without add-on parts, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Outer Surface Area
properties.definition: Total area of the surfaces of the object (not taking into account the end cap areas), normally generated as perimeter * length in case of extrusions.

properties.name: Assembly Place
properties.definition: Enumeration defining where the assembly is intended to take place, either in a factory, other offsite location or on the building site.

allowedValues.description: other offsite location or on the building site
other offsite location or on the building site
other offsite location or on the building site

properties.name: Tag
properties.definition: The tag (or label) identifier at the particular instance of a product, e.g. the serial number, or the position number. It is the identifier at the occurrence level.

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Object Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Description
properties.definition: Optional description, provided for exchanging informative comments.

properties.name: Global ID
properties.definition: Assignment of a globally unique identifier within the entire software world.

properties.name: Name
properties.definition: Optional name for use by the participating software systems or users. For some subtypes of [[IfcRoot]] the insertion of the [[Name]] attribute may be required. This would be enforced by a where rule.

IfcColumn.name: Column
IfcColumn.definition: An [[IfcColumn]] is a vertical structural or architectural member which often is aligned with a structural grid intersection. In most cases it represents a vertical, or nearly vertical, structural member that transmits, through compression, the weight of the structure above to other structural elements below. It may also represent such a member from an architectural point of view in which case it may represent a non load bearing element. Whether it is a structural load bearing element or a non-load bearing element is determined by the [[Pset_ColumnCommon]].[[LoadBearing]] property.

properties.name: Status
properties.definition: [[Status]] of the element, predominately used in renovation or retrofitting projects. The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition, 'Existing' - element exists and remains, 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished, 'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure).

allowedValues.description: 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished
The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition
'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure)

properties.name: Thermal Transmittance
properties.definition: Technical note: in IFC this property takes 'IfcThermalTransmittanceMeasure' as value. Such objects are not included in bSDD for simplicity reason. IFC also doesn't enforce particular units, but recommends using metric SI units (metre, kilogram, etc.). Read the IFC documentation for more information.

properties.name: Cross Section Area
properties.definition: Total area of the cross section (or profile) of the object.

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Gross Weight
properties.definition: Total Gross [[Weight]] of the object without any add-on parts and not taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Length
properties.definition: The length of the object.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Weight
properties.definition: Total net weight of the object without add-on parts, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Outer Surface Area
properties.definition: Total area of the surfaces of the object (not taking into account the end cap areas), normally generated as perimeter * length in case of extrusions.

properties.name: Assembly Place
properties.definition: Enumeration defining where the assembly is intended to take place, either in a factory, other offsite location or on the building site.

allowedValues.description: other offsite location or on the building site
other offsite location or on the building site
other offsite location or on the building site

properties.name: Tag
properties.definition: The tag (or label) identifier at the particular instance of a product, e.g. the serial number, or the position number. It is the identifier at the occurrence level.

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Object Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Description
properties.definition: Optional description, provided for exchanging informative comments.

properties.name: Global ID
properties.definition: Assignment of a globally unique identifier within the entire software world.

properties.name: Name
properties.definition: Optional name for use by the participating software systems or users. For some subtypes of [[IfcRoot]] the insertion of the [[Name]] attribute may be required. This would be enforced by a where rule.

IfcSpace.name: Space
IfcSpace.definition: A space represents an area or volume bounded actually or theoretically. Spaces are areas or volumes that provide for certain functions within a building.

properties.name: Downstream Connections
properties.definition: Names of downstream connected equipment and spaces, if not otherwise represented

properties.name: Process Capacity

properties.name: Process Item
properties.definition: The type of item (and its measurement method) being modelled within a process. This can be cargo, passengers or vehicles that pass through the system.

allowedValues.description: passengers or vehicles that pass through the system
passengers or vehicles that pass through the system

properties.name: Upstream Connections
properties.definition: Names of upstream connected equipment and spaces, if not otherwise represented

properties.name: Finish Ceiling Height
properties.definition: [[Height]] of the suspended ceiling (from top of flooring to the bottom of the suspended ceiling). To be provided only if the space has a suspended ceiling with constant height.

properties.name: Finish Floor Height
properties.definition: [[Height]] of the flooring (from base slab without flooring to the flooring height). To be provided only if the space has a constant flooring height.

properties.name: Gross Ceiling Area
properties.definition: Sum of all ceiling areas of the space. It includes the area covered by elementsinside the space (columns, inner walls, etc.). The ceiling area is the real (and not the projected) area (e.g. in case of sloped ceilings).

properties.name: Gross Floor Area
properties.definition: Sum of all gross floor areas within the spatial structure element.

properties.name: Gross Peri Meter
properties.definition: Gross perimeter at the outer contour of the object.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Gross Wall Area
properties.definition: Sum of all wall (and other vertically bounding elements, like columns) areas bounded by the space. It includes the area covered by elements inside the wall area (doors, windows, other openings, etc.).

properties.name: Height
properties.definition: Characteristic height

properties.name: Net Ceiling Area
properties.definition: Sum of all ceiling areas of the space. It excludes the area covered by elementsinside the space (columns, inner walls, etc.). The ceiling area is the real (and not the projected) area (e.g. in case of sloped ceilings).

properties.name: Net Floor Area
properties.definition: Sum of all net usable floor areas.

properties.name: Net Peri Meter
properties.definition: Net perimeter at the floor level of this space. It excludes those parts of the perimeter that are created by by virtual boundaries and openings (like doors). It is the measurement used for skirting boards and may include the perimeter of internal fixed objects like columns.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Wall Area
properties.definition: Sum of all wall (and other vertically bounding elements, like columns) areas bounded by the space. It excludes the area covered by elements inside the wall area (doors, windows, other openings, etc.).

properties.name: Elevation with Flooring
properties.definition: Level of flooring of this space; the average shall be taken, if the space ground surface is sloping or if there are level differences within this space.

properties.name: Long Name
properties.definition: Long name for the context as used for reference purposes.

properties.name: Relative Humidity
properties.definition: Loads from the relative humidity.

properties.name: Total Latent Load
properties.definition: Total energy added or removed from air that affects its humidity or concentration of water vapor. If a value is less than zero (negative), then the thermal load is lost from the space. If a value is greater than zero (positive), then the thermal load is a gain to the space.

properties.name: Total Radiant Load
properties.definition: Total electromagnetic energy added or removed by emission or absorption. If a value is less than zero (negative), then the thermal load is lost from the space. If a value is greater than zero (positive), then the thermal load is a gain to the space.

properties.name: Total Sensible Load
properties.definition: Total energy added or removed from air that affects its temperature. If a value is less than zero (negative), then the thermal load is lost from the space. If a value is greater than zero (positive), then the thermal load is a gain to the space.

properties.name: Ventilation Indoor Air
properties.definition: [[Ventilation]] loads from indoor air.

properties.name: Ventilation Outdoor Air
properties.definition: [[Ventilation]] loads from outdoor air.

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Object Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Description
properties.definition: Optional description, provided for exchanging informative comments.

properties.name: Global ID
properties.definition: Assignment of a globally unique identifier within the entire software world.

properties.name: Name
properties.definition: Optional name for use by the participating software systems or users. For some subtypes of [[IfcRoot]] the insertion of the [[Name]] attribute may be required. This would be enforced by a where rule.

IfcRoof.name: Roof
IfcRoof.definition: A roof is the covering of the top part of a building, it protects the building against the effects of weather.

properties.name: Status
properties.definition: [[Status]] of the element, predominately used in renovation or retrofitting projects. The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition, 'Existing' - element exists and remains, 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished, 'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure).

allowedValues.description: 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished
The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition
'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure)

properties.name: Gross Area
properties.definition: Gross [[Area]] of the object. Openings, recesses, projections and cut-outs are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Area
properties.definition: Total net area of the object. Openings, recesses and cut-outs are taken into account by subtraction, projections by addition.

properties.name: Projected Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the outer surfaces of the roof, projected tp the ground. It is the sum of all projected roof slab gross areas. Roof openings, like sky windows and other openings and cut-outs are not taken into account.

properties.name: Assembly Place
properties.definition: Enumeration defining where the assembly is intended to take place, either in a factory, other offsite location or on the building site.

allowedValues.description: other offsite location or on the building site
other offsite location or on the building site
other offsite location or on the building site

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Status
properties.definition: [[Status]] of the element, predominately used in renovation or retrofitting projects. The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition, 'Existing' - element exists and remains, 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished, 'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure).

allowedValues.description: 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Description

IfcRamp.name: Ramp
IfcRamp.definition: A ramp is a vertical passageway which provides a human or vehicle circulation link between one floor level and another floor level at a different elevation. It may include a landing as an intermediate floor slab. A ramp normally does not include steps.

properties.name: Status

The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition, 'Existing' - element exists and remains, 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished, 'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure).

properties.name: Element Type

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Object Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Description
properties.definition: Optional description, provided for exchanging informative comments.

properties.name: Global ID
properties.definition: Assignment of a globally unique identifier within the entire software world.

properties.name: Name
properties.definition: Optional name for use by the participating software systems or users. For some subtypes of [[IfcRoot]] the insertion of the [[Name]] attribute may be required. This would be enforced by a where rule.

IfcCurtainWall.name: Curtain Wall
IfcCurtainWall.definition: A curtain wall is a wall of a building which is an assembly of components, hung from the edge of the floor/roof structure rather than bearing on a floor. Curtain wall is represented as a building element assembly and implemented as a subtype of [[IfcBuiltElement]] that uses an IfcRelAggregates relationship. A curtain wall is often external, but using [[Pset_CurtainWallCommon]].[[IsExternal]] can be used to define interior curtain walls.

The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition, 'Existing' - element exists and remains, 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished, 'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure).

allowedValues.description: 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished
The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition
'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure)

properties.name: Tag
properties.definition: The tag (or label) identifier at the particular instance of a product, e.g. the serial number, or the position number. It is the identifier at the occurrence level.

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Object Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Description
properties.definition: Optional description, provided for exchanging informative comments.

properties.name: Global ID
properties.definition: Assignment of a globally unique identifier within the entire software world.

properties.name: Name
properties.definition: Optional name for use by the participating software systems or users. For some subtypes of [[IfcRoot]] the insertion of the [[Name]] attribute may be required. This would be enforced by a where rule.

IfcPlate.name: Plate
IfcPlate.definition: An [[IfcPlate]] is a planar and often flat part with constant thickness. A plate may carry loads between or beyond points of support, or provide stiffening. The location of the plate (being horizontal, vertical or sloped) is not relevant to its definition.

properties.name: Status
properties.definition: [[Status]] of the element, predominately used in renovation or retrofitting projects. The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition, 'Existing' - element exists and remains, 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished, 'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure).

allowedValues.description: 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished
The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition
'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure)

properties.name: Gross Area
properties.definition: Gross [[Area]] of the object. Openings, recesses, projections and cut-outs are not taken into account.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Gross Weight
properties.definition: Total Gross [[Weight]] of the object without any add-on parts and not taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Area
properties.definition: Total net area of the object. Openings, recesses and cut-outs are taken into account by subtraction, projections by addition.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Weight
properties.definition: Total net weight of the object without add-on parts, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Peri Meter
properties.definition: [[Perimeter]] of the object.

properties.name: Width
properties.definition: The width of the object. Only given, if the object has constant thickness (prismatic).

properties.name: Assembly Place
properties.definition: Enumeration defining where the assembly is intended to take place, either in a factory, other offsite location or on the building site.

allowedValues.description: other offsite location or on the building site
other offsite location or on the building site
other offsite location or on the building site

properties.name: Tag
properties.definition: The tag (or label) identifier at the particular instance of a product, e.g. the serial number, or the position number. It is the identifier at the occurrence level.

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Object Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Description
properties.definition: Optional description, provided for exchanging informative comments.

properties.name: Global ID
properties.definition: Assignment of a globally unique identifier within the entire software world.

properties.name: Name
properties.definition: Optional name for use by the participating software systems or users. For some subtypes of [[IfcRoot]] the insertion of the [[Name]] attribute may be required. This would be enforced by a where rule.

IfcMember.name: Member
IfcMember.definition: An [[IfcMember]] is a structural member designed to carry loads between or beyond points of support. It is not required to be load bearing. The orientation of the member (being horizontal, vertical or sloped) is not relevant to its definition (in contrary to [[IfcBeam]] and [[IfcColumn]]). An [[IfcMember]] represents a linear structural element from an architectural or structural modeling point of view and shall be used if it cannot be expressed more specifically as either an [[IfcBeam]] or an [[IfcColumn]].

properties.name: Status
properties.definition: [[Status]] of the element, predominately used in renovation or retrofitting projects. The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition, 'Existing' - element exists and remains, 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished, 'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure).

allowedValues.description: 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished
The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition
'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure)

properties.name: Cross Section Area
properties.definition: Total area of the cross section (or profile) of the object.

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Gross Weight
properties.definition: Total Gross [[Weight]] of the object without any add-on parts and not taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Length
properties.definition: The length of the object.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Weight
properties.definition: Total net weight of the object without add-on parts, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Outer Surface Area
properties.definition: Total area of the surfaces of the object (not taking into account the end cap areas), normally generated as perimeter * length in case of extrusions.

properties.name: Assembly Place
properties.definition: Enumeration defining where the assembly is intended to take place, either in a factory, other offsite location or on the building site.

allowedValues.description: other offsite location or on the building site
other offsite location or on the building site
other offsite location or on the building site

properties.name: Tag
properties.definition: The tag (or label) identifier at the particular instance of a product, e.g. the serial number, or the position number. It is the identifier at the occurrence level.

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Object Type

In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Description
properties.definition: Optional description, provided for exchanging informative comments.

properties.name: Global ID
properties.definition: Assignment of a globally unique identifier within the entire software world.

properties.name: Name
properties.definition: Optional name for use by the participating software systems or users. For some subtypes of [[IfcRoot]] the insertion of the [[Name]] attribute may be required. This would be enforced by a where rule.

IfcFooting.name: Footing
IfcFooting.definition: A footing is a part of the foundation of a structure that spreads and transmits the load to the soil. A footing is also characterized as shallow foundation, where the loads are transferred to the ground near the surface.

properties.name: Cross Section Area
properties.definition: Total area of the cross section (or profile) of the object.

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Gross Weight
properties.definition: Total Gross [[Weight]] of the object without any add-on parts and not taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Height
properties.definition: Characteristic height

properties.name: Length
properties.definition: The length of the object.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Weight
properties.definition: Total net weight of the object without add-on parts, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Outer Surface Area
properties.definition: Total area of the surfaces of the object (not taking into account the end cap areas), normally generated as perimeter * length in case of extrusions.

properties.name: Width
properties.definition: The width of the object. Only given, if the object has constant thickness (prismatic).

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. IfcPile.name: Pile
IfcPile.definition: A pile is a slender timber, concrete, or steel structural element, driven, jetted, or otherwise embedded on end in the ground for the purpose of supporting a load. A pile is also characterized as deep foundation, where the loads are transferred to deeper subsurface layers.

properties.name: Tag
properties.definition: The tag (or label) identifier at the particular instance of a product, e.g. the serial number, or the position number. It is the identifier at the occurrence level.

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Object Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute PredefinedType is set to [[USERDEFINED]] or when the concrete entity instantiated does not have a PredefinedType attribute. The latter is the case in some exceptional leaf classes and when instantiating [[IfcBuiltElement]] directly.

properties.name: Description
properties.definition: Optional description, provided for exchanging informative comments.

properties.name: Global ID
properties.definition: Assignment of a globally unique identifier within the entire software world.

properties.name: Name
properties.definition: Optional name for use by the participating software systems or users. For some subtypes of [[IfcRoot]] the insertion of the [[Name]] attribute may be required. This would be enforced by a where rule.

IfcRailing.name: Railing
IfcRailing.definition: The railing is a frame assembly adjacent to human or vehicle circulation spaces and at some space boundaries where it is used in lieu of walls or to complement walls. Designed as an optional physical support, or to prevent injury or damage, either by falling or collision.

properties.name: Status
properties.definition: [[Status]] of the element, predominately used in renovation or retrofitting projects. The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition, 'Existing' - element exists and remains, 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished, 'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure).

allowedValues.description: 'Demolish' - element existed but is to be demolished
The status can be assigned to as 'New' - element designed as new addition
'Temporary' - element will exists only temporary (like a temporary support structure)

properties.name: Element Type
properties.definition: The type denotes a particular type that indicates the object further. The use has to be established at the level of instantiable subtypes. In particular it holds the user defined type, if the enumeration of the attribute 'PredefinedType' is set to [[USERDEFINED]].

properties.name: Gross Surface Area
properties.definition: Total gross area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area. It is the sum of [[OuterSurfaceArea]] + (2 x [[CrossSectionArea]]) and shall only be given, if the [[OuterSurfaceArea]] and [[CrossSectionArea]] cannot be established separately.

properties.name: Gross Volume
properties.definition: Total gross volume of the object. Openings, recesses, enclosed objects and projections are not taken into account.

properties.name: Net Surface Area
properties.definition: Net surface area of the object, normally generated as perimeter * length + 2 * cross section area taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Net Volume
properties.definition: Total net volume of the object, taking into account possible processing features (cut-out's, etc.) or openings and recesses.

properties.name: Surface Genus After Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items after applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.

properties.name: Surface Genus Before Features
properties.definition: The Surface Genus of the evaluated representation items before applying product-level geometric features such as openings and projections.Surface Genus is a topological measure that represents the number of 'holes' or 'handles' on a surface. For example, a sphere has genus 0, and a torus has genus 1.
